author: parithosh
content: What were the short term tests you wanted us to have ready for ePBS <@755590043632140352> ?

author: potuz
content:
Yeah working on the list but the short ones will be mostly around reorged Payloads:

Range sync when a payload was reorged (not the block) 

Checkpoint sync when slot 0 was missed and the latest block 31 is both full or empty. 

Attempt to reorg a payload and see that the block is reorged

author: potuz
content: Those cover the most common cases that the chain should have right now to be able to work and quickly restart. The rest I'll send you a list of scenarios that we should test for hardness

author: m.kalinin
content:
A PR to the Engine API: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/786
Alternative to the one with the reorg depth limitation in the spec, PTAL

author: m.kalinin
content: btw, why `verify_execution_payload_envelope` doesn't do the `fee_recipient` check, i.e. the `fee_recipient` in the bid matches the one in the payload?

author: potuz
content: The Payload is a black box and the fee recipient in it can (and will) be different than in the bid

author: potuz
content: The fee recipient is where the funds of the on chain mechanism pays with a withdrawal, not where the builder sends money

author: m.kalinin
content: why then the `fee_recipient` is in the bid?

author: potuz
content: This

author: m.kalinin
content: So, the semantics of  the `fee_recipient` in the payload and in the bid are different, right?

author: potuz
content: Yes

author: m.kalinin
content: The payload's `fee_recipient` inherits pre-Gloas semantics, while in the bid we have a new field. I think it would be better to put a different name to that field, otherwise, it's confusing

author: potuz
content: But notice that trusted builders will continue using the same current semantics and either adjust Coinbase or send a tx on the EL to fee recipient, this is of no concern to the CL

author: potuz
content: I don't think so because of this dual way of paying, we support off protocol payments. Changing all the tooling to have different objects to deal with will be more confusing

author: m.kalinin
content: I see, it's a bit tricky, let me wrap my head around it

author: potuz
content: Already today fee recipient can be two different things on blocks

author: m.kalinin
content: I think today `execution_payload.fee_recipient` must match the `fee_recipient/coinbase/beneficiary` on the EL side, no?

author: potuz
content: Not necessarily, sometimes the builder sends a tx to the fee recipient

author: potuz
content: And keeps Coinbase to its own

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah usually the builder keeps the fee recipieint of the payload i.e coinbase to his own address and the last tx is usually a payment to the proposer of the slot i.e the value of the bid

author: potuz
content: Added this EIP to allow CL clients to sync without even requesting payloads, I think we can (and therefore should) prune all finalized envelopes and even on the unfinalized section we can sync without requesting payloads. https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11557

author: potuz
content: Could you guys please take a look? also awaiting for authorization to add authors 🙂
